name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **10Y AT 4.45%, CAPPED BY A SOFTER MONTHLY PCE — FED CUTS PRICED OUT. The US 10Y held 4.45% (+0.8% MoM, +10.0% 3M); the 2Y at 3.59%. April PCE (released 28 May) printed headline 3.8% YoY (a 3-year high) but monthly +0.4% vs +0.5% expected — a softer-than-feared monthly read that capped yields and dialed back the rate-HIKE bets that had been pressuring gold. Fed cuts remain fully priced OUT (the PPI/Warsh inflation regime, structural through Q3) — this does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. The softer monthly print, not the war, drove the gold rebound and the fresh US equity records.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **US 10Y 4.45% (-0.7% 1D, +2.2% MoM, +10.0% 3M) — REGIME NEAR 2026 HIGHS, BUT TODAY'S MOVE WAS CAPPED BY AN IN-LINE CORE PCE PRINT. The April PCE deflator released TODAY (May 28): headline PCE 3.8% (highest since May 2023), core PCE 3.3% (as expected, strongest since Oct). Because core came in-line — not a hot surprise — the yield reaction was muted (10Y -0.7% 1D), but the REGIME read is decisive: Fed cuts are now FULLY PRICED OUT and the market is beginning to price potential HIKES. (One source cited a 2026-high 4.67% intraday; the in-line core print likely capped the move — note as caveat.) The real-yield channel is the day's transmission engine: elevated real yields (oil↑→inflation↑→real-yields↑) pinned gold BELOW $4,400 to a 2-month low even on a live US-Iran ballistic-missile-exchange day — zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest decisively. US 2Y 3.59%. This is the PPI/Warsh regime doing double duty: capping gold and competing with EM-FX, structural through Q3 2026 and NOT reversible on a Hormuz reopening.** PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 (Wednesday May 27 — ~48h window; no brief ran May 26). **US 10Y 4.48% (~-0.2% 1D, +3.4% MoM, +13.1% 3M) — ELEVATED, AND IT IS THE CYCLE'S CLEANEST NON-CRISIS DRIVER. At 4.48% the 10Y is doing two things at once: (1) CAPPING GOLD — zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest, which is the proximate reason gold did NOT bid the May 25 strikes (see gold node C5); (2) COMPETING WITH EM-FX — elevated US real yields are a structural rupee headwind independent of oil (a steady FII/portfolio-outflow pull). The inflation-via-oil pass-through is STICKY: yields are +13.1% over 3M even as Brent de-prices ~-14% MoM, which says the bond market has banked the inflation pulse and does not expect it to fully reverse. The rate stress does NOT unwind on a Hormuz reopening — it is the PPI/Warsh regime, structural through Q3 2026. US 2Y 3.58% (curve still steep). VIX 16.7 cycle-low = no escalation priced. Next regime data: Friday May 30 PCE deflator (Fed's preferred gauge).** PRIOR (May 24): Day 86 (Sunday May 24 — markets closed; US Memorial Day Monday May 25). **US 10Y 4.56% FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (+5.4% MoM, +13.1% 3M). US 2Y 3.59% — 2s/10s = +97bps (steep). THE BOND MARKET IS NOT SPIKING ON OIL-INFLATION FEARS DESPITE OIL AT $103.54 — WELL ABOVE $73.80 PRE-CRISIS. NORMALLY A $30 OIL MOVE WOULD LIFT 10Y YIELDS 20-30BPS BUT 10Y IS UP ONLY +5.4% MoM. THE BOND MARKET IS PRICING THE INFLATION PULSE AS TRANSITORY — DEAL EXPECTATION EMBEDDED. IF DEAL FAILS, 10Y COULD SPIKE 20-40BPS QUICKLY AND THAT'S ITS OWN EQUITY HEADWIND. NEXT US DATA: TUESDAY MAY 26 CONFERENCE BOARD CONSUMER CONFIDENCE + CASE-SHILLER; FRIDAY MAY 30 PCE DEFLATOR (FED'S PREFERRED INFLATION GAUGE).** PCE Friday is the next regime-relevant print. **Reversibility**: deal-failure case re-prices 10Y +20-40bps within 5-10 sessions; durable PPI-Warsh regime keeps 10Y in 4.50-4.65% band. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.09%** flat into Monday Indian-only session; RBI Tuesday May 26 $5B 3Y swap auction is the institutional inflection. PRIOR (May 23): US 10Y 4.56% MAY 22 CLOSE — WARSH REGIME PERSISTENCE AT HIGHER LEVEL; APRIL MINUTES (WED MAY 20) SHOWED HISTORIC DISSENT COUNT. US 2Y 3.59% (+0.1% 1D, -0.3% 1M, -0.1% 3M) — FLAT, FED PRICING AT NEUTRAL HOLD.** **Primary chain (~60% inflation regime intact)**: April PPI +1.4% m/m + April CPI 3.8% YoY structural through Q3 2026; Warsh effective May 16 — Fed easing path closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. Fed April minutes May 20 'important event for new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh' (Kiplinger) — historic dissent count from FOMC hold; bond market read: hawkish dissent supports steeper curve. **Secondary chain (~25% deal-anticipation)**: Brent flat-to-down 1M (-1.5%) + WTI broke $100 to $96.60 → oil pass-through to PPI compression (modest, not durable); deal-anticipation tape removed marginal inflation-cascade overhang. **Tertiary chain (~15% Nvidia earnings risk-on)**: Nvidia Q1 earnings beat + AI-cycle bid pulled risk-on flows into equity, allowing 10Y to ease modestly. **Cross-asset confirmation**: VIX 16.70 cycle low + S&P 7,473.47 +0.4% + 10Y eased + Gold $4,521 underperforming on real-yield channel + deal-anticipation amplification. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00%** (paused near 7.00% after 4-session advance per Trading Economics May 22; -0.03 to -0.07pp est. WoW) — eased on RBI swap-auction announcement + government bond-tax-cut study. **DXY 99.32** +0.1% 1D / +0.5% 1M / +1.7% 3M — broadly stable. **Reversibility**: Crisis component (~25%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure (10Y bounces back); inflation regime (~60%) does NOT reverse — structural through Q3 2026; Warsh transition (~15%) takes ~90 days to clarify. **Next data**: May PPI/CPI cycle (June 13 schedule per BLS) — until then yields oscillate ~4.50-4.65%. **Decision-relevant**: 10Y easing into Nvidia print is NOT a peace-dividend signal; it's a temporary risk-on flow. The PPI-Warsh regime is the binding force. PRIOR (May 22): **US 10Y 4.56% -0.5% 1D / +5.5% 1M / +13.2% 3M — EASING INTO NVIDIA-EARNINGS RISK-ON; PPI-WARSH REGIME INTACT AT HIGHER LEVEL. US 2Y 3.58% 0.0% 1D / -0.4% 1M / -0.2% 3M.** **Primary chain (~60% inflation regime intact)**: April PPI +1.4% m/m + April CPI 3.8% YoY structural through Q3 2026; Warsh effective May 16 — Fed easing path closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. **Secondary chain (~25% deal-anticipation)**: Brent crash -5.3% across window + WTI broke $100 to $99.50 → oil pass-through to PPI compression (modest, not durable); deal-anticipation tape removed a marginal inflation-cascade overhang. **Tertiary chain (~15% Nvidia earnings risk-on)**: Nvidia Q1 earnings beat + AI-cycle bid pulled risk-on flows into equity, allowing 10Y to ease modestly. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-regime + Nvidia-earnings risk-on**: VIX 16.61 (cycle low) + S&P/NASDAQ +0.6%/+0.4% + 10Y -0.5% + Gold -0.5% (gold still underperforming on real-yield channel + deal-anticipation amplification). **India 10Y G-Sec 7.07% -0.03pp 1D** eased on RBI swap-auction announcement + government bond-tax-cut study; foreign-investor channel partial relief. **DXY 99.23 0.0% 1D / +0.4% 1M / +1.6% 3M** broadly stable; DXY-INR decoupling on RBI defense. **Reversibility**: Crisis component (~25%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure (10Y bounces back); inflation regime (~60%) does NOT reverse — structural through Q3 2026 at minimum; Warsh transition (~15%) takes ~90 days to clarify. **Next regime data**: May PPI/CPI cycle (June 13 / June 13 schedule per BLS) — until then, yields oscillate ~4.50-4.65%. **Decision-relevant**: 10Y easing into Nvidia print is NOT a peace-dividend signal; it's a temporary risk-on flow. The PPI-Warsh regime is the binding force. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **US 10Y 4.59% (carries forward Friday close, +3.0% cumulative Fri+Mon, +6.6% 1M, +13.4% 3M); US 2Y 3.59% flat; US 30Y ~5.12% (flat-to-up vs Friday, 10-month high)** per markets dossier Section A. Bloomberg 'worst weekly Treasury rout in a year' framing carries from Friday. **Primary chain (~70% rates regime)**: April PPI +1.4% m/m (BLS May 13, biggest since Dec 2022) + Warsh effective May 16 (rate-hike-before-year-end now in CNBC/Yahoo discussion) — Fed easing path closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027; rate-cut bets now sit beyond 2026. **Secondary chain (~30% crisis)**: Brent $111.47 +2.0% Monday (StanChart $130-145 path activates same day) → oil pass-through to PPI intensifies → producer-side inflation feed-through to Treasury yields. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.05-7.10% flat** (+6-8 bp 1M, +13 bp 3M); investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; benchmark 6.48% 2035 bond yield ~7.05%; FII debt outflows continuing. **Cross-asset stagflation regime intact**: 10Y +14 bp Friday + DXY 99.22 firm + Gold -0.4% (real-yield channel competing) + VIX 18.43 sub-20 + S&P -1.24% Friday. **Tuesday May 19 NSC binary**: strike orders → yields gap higher again (energy pass-through intensifies); back-off → yields hold or modestly retrace; nothing operational → yields oscillate. **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE YIELDS ARE FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSES: US 10Y 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025); US 30Y 5.121% (highest since May 2025).** Per markets dossier Section A: India 10Y G-Sec 7.09% (+5 bps 1D May 15) — rebound from prior decline; CCIL monthly avg 6.95% vs 7.04% prior; RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15. **STAGFLATION REGIME UNCHANGED**: April PPI +1.4% m/m + April CPI 3.8% YoY closed Fed easing path indefinitely; Warsh effective May 16. **Monday May 18 open is the test** — if NYT preparation report is fully priced into Brent and oil gaps higher, yields move higher again; if markets discount the report as posturing, yields hold. **Tertiary chain (~15% Iran) intensifies**: Trump 2-month ultimatum letter + NYT preparation report adds tail-risk premium. Reversibility unchanged: crisis component (~15%) reverses if Iran responds within 2-month window; PPI/CPI inflation regime (~60%) does NOT reverse — structural through Q3 2026 at minimum; Warsh transition (~25%) takes ~90 days to clarify; risk asymmetric to hawkish surprise. **PRIOR (May 16):** STAGFLATION BOND ROUT — US 10Y +14bp to 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025); US 30Y +11bp to 5.121% (highest since May 2025) — Bloomberg labelled it 'the worst weekly Treasury rout in a year.'** Primary chain (~60% inflation): **April PPI +1.4% m/m / +6% YoY (BLS May 13)** confirms producer-side inflation has fully absorbed the energy shock — Core PPI +1% (vs 0.4% est), ~60% of rise was in services, energy at the root; combined with April CPI 3.8% YoY (May 12) Fed easing path is closed indefinitely; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027 earlier in the week; CNBC/Yahoo reporting markets now consider rate hike possible. Secondary chain (~25% Fed transition): **Warsh confirmed 54-45 May 13 replacing Powell effective today May 16**; Warsh's preference for trimmed mean inflation measures = sees underlying inflation cooler than headline, but Warsh shrinks balance sheet + relies on rate tool = potentially more hawkish on QE channel; markets uncertain about reaction function for first 90 days = term premium expanding. Tertiary chain (~15% Iran): Trump 2-month ultimatum letter creates a defined uncertainty window; oil pass-through to PPI documented and ongoing. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (flat-to-easing)**: traders sidelined ahead of Trump-Xi summit; RBI bond-tax cut for foreigners under consideration; RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15. **Cross-asset stagflation confirmation**: 10Y +14bp / 30Y +11bp / DXY +0.4% / Silver -9.1% / Gold -2.6% / S&P -1.24% / VIX 17.26→18.43 / WTI +4.2%. The paper-equity leg of the cycle-extreme bifurcation reversed within 24 hours toward crisis confirmation. PRIOR (May 15): **US 10Y 4.46% MAY 15 (-0.4% 1D, +4.2% 1M, +10.1% 3M); US 2Y 3.59% (-0.3% 1D, -0.7% 1M, -0.1% 3M).** Front-end says no near-term cut, no near-term hike either. **April CPI 3.8% YoY + April PPI +1.4% m/m closed near-term Fed easing door**; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. Real-yield channel keeps gold in regime-shift unwind (gold -2.2% 1D); DXY 99.13 (+0.3% 1D) — modest dollar bid on Trump-Xi joint statement. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-regime + paper-physical bifurcation at extreme**: VIX 17.26 cycle low + S&P/NASDAQ/Dow record closes + 10Y at 4.46% holding all reinforce paper-side complacency on a day with two new kinetic incidents and an Israeli KIA. India 10Y G-Sec 7.12% (DealPlexus / CCIL May 14 — yield easing as traders awaited Trump-Xi summit outcome; CCIL monthly avg 6.95% vs 7.04% prior). **US 10Y 4.48% MAY 14 (+0.4% 1D, +5.3% 1M, +10.5% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, -0.3% 1M, +0.2% 3M).** CPI absorption — Fed easing closed (April CPI 3.8% three-year high May 12; energy +17.9% YoY contributed >40% headline gain; core +2.8% YoY). Front-end says no near-term cut, no near-term hike either. **Real-yield channel keeps gold underperforming** ($4,701.50 +0.1% 1D — modestly underperforming bear case on Trump-Xi opening). India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (month high — investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; Moody's sovereign-cut absorbed; +bps vs recent 6.96% range). DXY 98.48 (-0.0% 1D, +0.4% 1M, +1.4% 3M) — dollar failing to bid on crisis day (G5). **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime: VIX 17.87 sub-18 + S&P 500 +0.6% + 10Y at 4.48% holding all reinforce that markets see crisis as INFLATION + FLOW event** (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT financial-system stress event. PRIOR (May 9): **US 10Y 4.36% MAY 8 CLOSE (-0.6% 1D, +1.7% 1M, +4.0% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, +0.2% 1M).** Modest 10Y pullback on stronger-than-expected May 8 jobs print (per Reuters/Investing 'labor market resilience') reduced immediate June-cut probability; longer-end yields easing back from FOMC-driven highs. **Real-yield channel still keeping gold underperforming:** US 10Y at 4.36% kept real yields competitive vs gold; gold $4,720.40 (+0.4% 1D) underperformed on a crisis-day with May 7 destroyer attacks + May 8 second tanker disabling + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' speech. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime:** VIX cycle-low 17.08 + S&P 500 record close 7,398.93 + 10Y not making new highs all reinforce that markets see the West Asia crisis as an INFLATION + FLOW event (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT a financial-system stress event (which would be positive for gold + bid for 10Y). Cheniere $3.5B Q1 GAAP loss on derivative MtM is the closest thing to a financial-stress signal but is firm-specific not systemic. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0% (+6-8 bps cycle, ~+15bps from late-Apr lows, ~+25bps from pre-crisis) — rupee weakness + oil inflation + FII channel keeping pressure. DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M) modest dollar weakness. Powell verbal 'hasn't even peaked yet' on energy inflation still operative; CME FedWatch June-cut probability now low.

current.driver: Soft April monthly PCE (+0.4% vs +0.5% exp) capped the 10Y; Fed cuts fully priced out (PPI/Warsh inflation regime, structural through Q3). Does not reverse on a Hormuz deal.

top_edges.context: 2026-05-27 (markets dossier C5): US 10Y at 4.48% is the proximate cap on gold — zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest against elevated real yields, which (with a firm DXY) is why gold did NOT bid the May 25 strikes (-0.4%). This is the inverse-relationship channel that defines whether the crisis bids gold or the dollar. First explicit activation as a distinct yield→gold edge (the gold↔fed channel already carries the rate-expectations leg).

recent_signals.headline: **10Y AT 4.45%, CAPPED BY A SOFTER MONTHLY PCE — FED CUTS PRICED OUT.** The 10Y held 4.45% (+0.8% MoM), 2Y 3.59%. April PCE (28 May) printed headline 3.8% YoY (3-yr high) but monthly +0.4% vs +0.5% expected — a softer read that capped yields and dialed back the rate-HIKE bets pressuring gold. Cuts remain fully priced out (PPI/Warsh inflation regime, structural through Q3); does not reverse on a Hormuz deal.
recent_signals.verification: 10Y 4.45% / 2Y 3.59% = CONFIRMED (data); April PCE 3.8% YoY / +0.4% monthly = CONFIRMED; cuts priced out = market-implied.

recent_signals.headline: **US 10Y 4.45% (-0.7% 1D, +2.2% MoM, +10.0% 3M) — REGIME NEAR 2026 HIGHS, BUT TODAY'S MOVE CAPPED BY AN IN-LINE CORE PCE; FED CUTS FULLY PRICED OUT, HIKES BEING PRICED.** (1) **April PCE released TODAY (May 28)**: headline PCE 3.8% (highest since May 2023), core PCE 3.3% (as expected, strongest since Oct). Because core printed IN-LINE — not a hot surprise — the yield reaction was muted (10Y -0.7% 1D); but the regime read is decisive: the Fed easing path is closed indefinitely and the market is now beginning to price potential HIKES rather than cuts. (2) **The 2026-high caveat**: one source cited an intraday 2026-high of 4.67%; the in-line core print likely capped the move back to 4.45% — flag as a data caveat, not a confirmed close. (3) **Real-yield channel is the day's transmission engine**: elevated real yields (oil↑→inflation↑→real-yields↑) pinned gold BELOW $4,400 to a 2-month low even on a day with a LIVE US-Iran ballistic-missile exchange (IRGC fired one missile at a US base in Kuwait, intercepted, zero casualty — calibrated). Zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest decisively; the safe-haven flow did NOT go to gold. (4) US 2Y 3.59% (curve steep). **This is the PPI/Warsh regime doing double duty — capping gold and competing with EM-FX — structural through Q3 2026 and NOT reversible on a Hormuz reopening.** India 10Y G-Sec ~7.03% STALE (last live May 25 — needs CCIL refresh).
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-28 Section A Bonds (US 10Y 4.45% -0.7% 1D / +2.2% MoM / +10.0% 3M; US 2Y 3.59%) + C7/D

Markets dossier 2026-05-28 (real-yield channel pinned gold below $4,400 to a 2-month low; 2026-high 4.67% caveat — in-line core likely capped the move)
recent_signals.verification: US 10Y 4.45% / 2Y 3.59% = CONFIRMED operational tape (yfinance, markets dossier A); April PCE headline 3.8% / core 3.3% = CONFIRMED (BLS/BEA primary release May 28); cuts-priced-out / hikes-being-priced = CONFIRMED market pricing; real-yield suppression of gold below $4,400 = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS supported by confirmed gold-tape; 4.67% intraday 2026-high = REPORTED CAVEAT (single source, likely capped by in-line core).

recent_signals.headline: **US 10Y 4.48% (~-0.2% 1D, +3.4% MoM, +13.1% 3M) — ELEVATED; THE CLEANEST NON-CRISIS DRIVER, DOING DOUBLE DUTY: CAPPING GOLD AND COMPETING WITH EM-FX.** (1) **Real-yield suppression of gold**: at 4.48% the 10Y is the proximate reason gold did NOT bid the May 25 US strikes / Lebanon ground op — zero-yield gold loses the opportunity-cost contest, and the safe-haven flow went into the DOLLAR (DXY 99.22) instead (see gold node C5). (2) **EM-FX competition**: elevated US real yields are a structural rupee headwind INDEPENDENT of oil — a steady portfolio-outflow pull that helps explain why India refused the oil-relief bounce (rupee back to ₹95.68). (3) **Sticky pass-through**: yields are +13.1% over 3M even as Brent de-prices ~-14% MoM — the bond market has banked the inflation-via-oil pulse and does not expect it to fully reverse; the PPI/Warsh regime is structural through Q3 2026 and does NOT unwind on a Hormuz reopening. (4) US 2Y 3.58% (curve steep); VIX 16.7 cycle-low = no escalation priced. **Next regime data: Friday May 30 PCE deflator (Fed's preferred inflation gauge).** India 10Y G-Sec ~7.03% STALE (last live May 25 — needs CCIL refresh).

recent_signals.headline: **US 10Y 4.62% (+9% 1M) / US 30Y ~5.13% (10-MONTH HIGH, +30bp 3M) — BLOOMBERG 'WORST WEEKLY TREASURY ROUT IN A YEAR' STILL OPERATIVE.** US 2Y 3.57% (-0.6% 1D) — hold cuts priced. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.10-7.13% (flat to +5-7 bp; six-week high on crude + UST). **The rate stress is the cleanest non-crisis driver — does NOT reverse on Hormuz reopening.** Drivers: PPI +1.4% m/m hot, Warsh effective May 16, oil pass-through (partial crisis-link), inflation-persistence framing from Fed officials. S&P/NASDAQ flat-to-down marginally despite postponement = rates overriding geopolitics.
